text: Fill in the blanks about the face and parotid region.
questions: All muscles of facial expression are derived from the 2nd pharyngeal arch and are supplied by the *facial/CN VII:cranial nerve that exits through the stylomastoid foramen* nerve.
In an upper motor neuron (UMN) facial palsy, the *forehead/frontalis:part of face that is spared* is spared because it has bilateral cortical representation.

The five terminal branches of the facial nerve within the parotid gland are remembered by the mnemonic: To Zanzibar By Motor Car — *Temporal:first branch*, Zygomatic, Buccal, Marginal mandibular, and Cervical.
The deep facial vein connects the *pterygoid:venous plexus in the infratemporal fossa* venous plexus to the facial vein, providing a route for infection to reach the cavernous sinus.
Frey's syndrome occurs after *parotidectomy:surgical removal of the parotid gland* due to aberrant regeneration of parasympathetic secretomotor fibres to sweat glands in the skin.

overallFeedback.feedback: Keep studying — review the material and try again.
